commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=997f46c3df9ad059de6db1ff643e69e9b380f76c)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Codex app-server startup cleanup so shared clients survive startup app errors, adds focused cleanup tests, and updates a few bundled plugin `NaN` literals to `Number.NaN`.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +203. Total +213 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR-introduced bug: source inspection shows a `thread/start` JSON-RPC response error ending in ` timed out` is wrapped as an app error, then the PR's suffix check clears the shared client. I did not run a live invalid bearer-token path, and the PR body says that live path was not tested.
commands.review_followup: Needs author or maintainer follow-up for the code finding and real behavior proof; the protected maintainer label also makes this human-handled rather than a cleanup close.

target.title: fix(codex): preserve shared app-server after startup app errors
target.body:
Summary
- Keep the shared Codex app-server alive when thread startup fails with an app-level error, such as an auth failure from thread/start.
- Still clear the shared app-server for abandoned startup work: transport close, startup timeout, cancellation, and per-RPC startup timeouts.
- Add direct startup cleanup regressions around the shared-client lease path.
- Clean up current bundled-extension `Number.NaN` lint nits that blocked the Codex extension lint lane.

Verification
- git diff --check
- ../codex contract check: codex-rs/app-server/README.md documents one app-server process serving thread/start, thread/resume, thread/fork, and thread/loaded/list, with threads remaining loaded after unsubscribe.
- Testbox-through-Crabbox provider=blacksmith-testbox id=tbx_01ksng3jywgk2n32yqbgn2y5wm: corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt-thread-cleanup.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts => 3 files, 26 tests passed on rebased branch.
- Testbox-through-Crabbox provider=blacksmith-testbox id=tbx_01ksnhbwd97jhkwejt2crmrtgf: corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && corepack pnpm lint:extensions:bundled && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt-thread-cleanup.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts => bundled extension lint passed; 3 files, 26 tests passed.
- Testbox-through-Crabbox provider=blacksmith-testbox id=tbx_01ksnh2yfx243mn8a9cxy28431: corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions:test passed before the lint-only cleanup.
- autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

Behavior addressed: Helper/child Codex startup app errors no longer kill the parent session's shared Codex app-server.
Real environment tested: Local macOS source review plus Linux Testbox-through-Crabbox focused Vitest/lint/type lanes with a mocked Codex app-server transport.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: git diff --check; source-contract check in ../codex; Testbox commands listed above; autoreview branch review against origin/main.
Evidence after fix: Regression keeps stdin open after thread/start returns an auth-style JSON-RPC app error, and closes stdin only for timeout, cancellation, and per-RPC timeout cleanup paths.
Observed result after fix: One shared app-server client is preserved for logical app errors and only restarted when the startup request has been abandoned or the transport is poisoned.
What was not tested: A live invalid bearer token against the real Codex API was not exercised; the failure shape is covered by the app-server JSON-RPC error contract.

Fixes #72574.
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
